edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The post-trained BF16 model is initialized from the Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B Base BF16 checkpoint (pretrained on 25T tokens with NVFP4) and then sequentially SFT'd, RLVR'd, SWE-RL'd, RLHF'd, and MTP-healed.

evidence.excerpt: Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B BF16 : post-trained model • Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B Base BF16 : base model
evidence.explanation: The technical report enumerates both checkpoints in the open release; the BF16 post-trained checkpoint is explicitly distinguished from the Base BF16 it was post-trained on.

evidence.excerpt: NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16 (pre-trained checkpoint)
evidence.explanation: The HF card lists the base BF16 checkpoint as the initialization for this post-trained model.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Aggregator-level edge: the post-trained model used the Nemotron-Super-Post-Training-Data collection (an SFT + RL aggregator mix) for the SFT, RLVR, SWE-RL and RLHF stages described in §3 of the tech report.

evidence.excerpt: Nemotron-Super-Post-Training-Data : a collection of RL environments and SFT datasets targeting a broad range of agentic capabilities
evidence.explanation: Authoritatively names the post-training aggregator mix released alongside the model.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 (synthetic code-concepts, unconditional algorithmic, economics, formal logic, and MCQ corpora) was added to pretraining for Nemotron 3 Super and propagates through the post-trained checkpoint via the base initialization.

evidence.excerpt: We are releasing these datasets on HuggingFace as Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1.
evidence.explanation: The tech report names the specialized pretraining release added for Nemotron 3 Super.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: The base model was pretrained on Nemotron-CC v2 and v2.1 (≈9.13T tokens of English Common Crawl partitioned into the five quality-based groups crawl-medium, crawl-medium-high, crawl-high and their synthetic counterparts).

evidence.excerpt: we partition into five quality-based groups following the Nemotron-CC taxonomy (Su et al., 2025): crawl-medium, crawl-medium-high, and crawl-high
evidence.explanation: Names Nemotron-CC as the source of the largest pretraining component.

evidence.location: Model card, Pre-Training Base Corpus table
evidence.excerpt: Nemotron-CC-v2 & v2.1 | 9.13T | English web data from Common Crawl
evidence.explanation: HF card quantifies the Nemotron-CC contribution.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron-CC-Math-v1 (133.3B-token math corpus that preserves LaTeX) is part of the pretraining mixture's math component (cited as Mahabadi et al., 2025).

evidence.excerpt: the mixture includes math (Mahabadi et al., 2025; Akter et al., 2024)
evidence.explanation: Mahabadi et al. 2025 is the Nemotron-CC-Math paper; the citation places it in the math slice of the pretraining mixture.

evidence.excerpt: Nemotron-CC-Math-v1 | 133.3B | Math with LaTeX preservation
evidence.explanation: HF card lists Nemotron-CC-Math-v1 as a base pretraining ingredient.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron-CC-Code-v1 (≈428B tokens of high-quality code from Common Crawl, extracted via Lynx + LLM pipeline) is in the pretraining code slice.

evidence.excerpt: Nemotron-CC-Code-v1 | 427.9B | High-quality code from Common Crawl using Lynx + LLM pipeline
evidence.explanation: HF card lists Nemotron-CC-Code-v1 in the base pretraining corpus.

evidence.excerpt: Wikipedia, code, Nemotron-CC-Code, academic text
evidence.explanation: Tech report enumerates Nemotron-CC-Code as one of the named pretraining components.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron-Pretraining-Code-v1 (curated GitHub code with multi-stage filtering and synthetic code data) is part of the base pretraining mix's code slice (~1.09T tokens combined with v2).

evidence.excerpt: Nemotron-Pretraining-Code-v1 & v2 | 1.09T | Curated GitHub code with multi-stage filtering and synthetic code data
evidence.explanation: HF card enumerates Nemotron-Pretraining-Code-v1/v2 as base pretraining ingredients.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: Leaf-level edge: the base model trained on Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 — a synthetic STEM/code-concepts/economics/formal-logic/MCQ corpus newly added for Nemotron 3 Super.

evidence.excerpt: We describe here several new datasets that we added to pretraining since Nemotron 3 Nano (NVIDIA, 2025a). We are releasing these datasets on HuggingFace as Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1.
evidence.explanation: Authoritative naming of the specialized release as a base pretraining ingredient.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: FinePDFs (Kydlíček et al., 2025) is one of the named non-NVIDIA pretraining ingredients in the base mixture (~6.1% of Phase 1 and 14.3% of Phase 2 contains finepdfs/finepdfs-high).

evidence.excerpt: multilingual data, finepdfs (Kydlíček et al., 2025) and synthetic SFT-style datasets
evidence.explanation: Tech report names finepdfs in the pretraining blend; Figure 10 shows its phase weights.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: FineWeb-2 is listed as one of the non-NVIDIA public pretraining datasets used in the base corpus (multilingual web).

evidence.excerpt: datasets/HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-2 (FineWeb-2)
evidence.explanation: HF card explicitly enumerates HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-2 as a public dataset used in pretraining.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: FineMath is one of the named external math corpora in the base pretraining mix.

evidence.excerpt: datasets/HuggingFaceTB/finemath (FineMath)
evidence.explanation: HF card explicitly enumerates HuggingFaceTB/finemath as a public dataset used in pretraining.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: OpenWebMath is enumerated as a named public ingredient of the base pretraining corpus.

evidence.excerpt: datasets/open-web-math/open-web-math (OpenWebMath)
evidence.explanation: HF card explicitly lists open-web-math/open-web-math under the public pretraining datasets.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: AllenAI peS2o (academic / scientific text) is enumerated in the base pretraining public-datasets list.

evidence.excerpt: datasets/allenai/peS2o
evidence.explanation: HF card explicitly enumerates allenai/peS2o as a public pretraining dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: The MMLU auxiliary training split is used as the seed for the synthetic multiple-choice (MMLU-aux-train-SDG) corpus that contributed ~1.6B tokens to base pretraining.

evidence.excerpt: We construct a multiple-choice question (MCQ) dataset by bootstrapping from the MMLU auxiliary training set (Hendrycks et al., 2021b)
evidence.explanation: MMLU auxiliary train is the explicit seed source for an in-mix synthetic pretraining dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: GSM8K is enumerated in the public pretraining datasets and is used as a seed for synthetic STEM/math content during base pretraining.

evidence.excerpt: datasets/openai/grade-school-math (GSM8K)
evidence.explanation: HF card explicitly enumerates openai/grade-school-math (GSM8K) as a public pretraining dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron-PrismMath (4.6B tokens) was added to the base pretraining math/synthetic component (sourced from Big-Math-RL-Verified and OpenR1-Math-220k seeds).

evidence.excerpt: Nemotron-PrismMath | 4.6B | Big-Math-RL-Verified, OpenR1-Math-220k | Qwen2.5-0.5B, Qwen2.5-72B, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B
evidence.explanation: HF card lists Nemotron-PrismMath as part of the synthetic pretraining datasets.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron-MIND (Math-Informed syNthetic Dialogues, Akter et al., 2024) is part of the math slice of the pretraining mixture.

evidence.excerpt: the mixture includes math (Mahabadi et al., 2025; Akter et al., 2024)
evidence.explanation: Akter et al. 2024 is the MIND paper; the citation places MIND in the pretraining math slice.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: GPT-OSS-120B generated five candidate Python solutions per problem for the Synthetic Code Concepts pretraining dataset (~23M problem–solution pairs), generated rewrites/scoring for the Synthetic Unconditional Algorithmic data, and was a generator for the Synthetic Multiple-Choice and other pretraining datasets.

evidence.excerpt: we then used GPT-OSS 120B to generate five self-contained solutions for each generated problem
evidence.explanation: Names GPT-OSS-120B as the solution generator for the Synthetic Code Concepts pretraining dataset.

evidence.excerpt: We further used gpt-oss-120b to score the correctness of the solution and, if incorrect, to correct it
evidence.explanation: GPT-OSS-120B also scored/corrected algorithmic Python problems for the unconditional algorithmic dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: GPT-OSS-20B generated ≈14M Python programming problems (with descriptive function names and docstrings) for the Synthetic Code Concepts pretraining dataset.

evidence.excerpt: we performed open-ended generation using GPT-OSS 20B to generate Python programming problems
evidence.explanation: Names GPT-OSS-20B as the problem generator for Synthetic Code Concepts.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 generated and verified the Synthetic Economics MCQ dataset and the Synthetic Formal Logic dataset that fed pretraining.

evidence.excerpt: we used Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 to generate multiple questions, each accompanied by a detailed, step-by-step, and well-formatted solution
evidence.explanation: Names Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 as the generator for Synthetic Economics.

evidence.excerpt: We generated and evaluated the problems and solutions using Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507
evidence.explanation: Same model also generated the Synthetic Formal Logic pretraining dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: Qwen3-235B-A22B (the base/non-thinking model) generated MMLU-style MCQ candidate questions for the Synthetic Multiple Choice pretraining dataset and authored algorithmic Python problems for the Synthetic Unconditional Algorithmic pretraining dataset.

evidence.excerpt: we generate multiple similar questions ... by prompting Qwen3-235B-A22B (Yang et al., 2025)
evidence.explanation: Names Qwen3-235B-A22B as the MCQ generator for the synthetic MMLU-style pretraining dataset.

evidence.excerpt: we generated algorithmic Python problems using Qwen3-235B-A22B (the base model) and gpt-oss-120b
evidence.explanation: Qwen3-235B-A22B is named as a generator for the Synthetic Unconditional Algorithmic pretraining set.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: DeepSeek-V3 was used in the second stage of the Synthetic Multiple Choice pretraining pipeline to solve and majority-vote candidate MCQs, deciding which question/answer pairs were retained for training.

evidence.excerpt: we prompt the DeepSeek-V3 (DeepSeek-AI, 2025c) model to solve each generated question by selecting an answer
evidence.explanation: DeepSeek-V3 is the inclusion judge (majority-voting solver) for the Synthetic MCQ pretraining dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: Qwen3-Embedding-0.6 was used to embed Synthetic Unconditional Algorithmic problems/solutions and remove any with >0.8 cosine similarity to HumanEval/MBPP/CRUXEval/LiveCodeBench (decontamination filter).

evidence.excerpt: we used Qwen3-Embedding-0.6 to encode Problem and Solution and filtered any data with >0.8 similarity with any of the benchmarks
evidence.explanation: Documents Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B as the decontamination filter on a pretraining synthetic code dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: DeepSeek-V3.2 acted as a teacher when refreshing SFT datasets (Competition Math, Competition Code, Conversational Tool Use, Multilingual, Science), as the primary engine generating step-by-step Terminal-Use solution traces in dockerized environments, and as one of the generators in the Conversational Tool Use and General-Purpose Tool Use SFT pipelines.

evidence.excerpt: We refresh the following datasets with new teacher models (DeepSeek v3.2, Kimi K2): Competition Math, Competition Code, Conversational Tool Use, Multilingual, Science.
evidence.explanation: Names DeepSeek-V3.2 as a refreshed-SFT teacher across five subject areas.

evidence.excerpt: we use DeepSeek-V3.2 (DeepSeek-AI, 2025a) as the primary engine to generate step-by-step solution traces within isolated, Dockerized environments
evidence.explanation: Names DeepSeek-V3.2 as the primary trajectory generator for the Terminal-Use SFT dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Kimi K2 was used as a refreshed teacher model when regenerating the Competition Math, Competition Code, Conversational Tool Use, Multilingual, and Science SFT datasets.

evidence.excerpt: We refresh the following datasets with new teacher models (DeepSeek v3.2, Kimi K2)
evidence.explanation: Names Kimi K2 as a co-teacher for the refreshed SFT datasets.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B-Instruct was the SFT teacher for the Software Engineering dataset: it regenerated R2E-Gym problem statements and produced distillation trajectories from the OpenHands harness, and also acted as one of the agentic-LLM trajectory sources for Agentic-CLI SFT.

evidence.excerpt: We distill trajectories from the OpenHands agent harness using Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B-Instruct as the teacher model
evidence.explanation: Names Qwen3-Coder-480B as the SWE-SFT teacher distilled from.

evidence.excerpt: we distill from high-performance, open-source agentic LLMs such as Qwen-3-Coder-480B (Qwen, 2025) and Minimax M2.5
evidence.explanation: Qwen3-Coder-480B is also a CLI agentic-trajectory teacher.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: MiniMax-M2 generated CLI interaction trajectories (alongside Qwen3-Coder-480B) for the Agentic-Programming SFT dataset and solved the obfuscated multi-hop search queries (via Tavily MCP) that produced the Search-agent SFT trajectories.

evidence.excerpt: we distill from high-performance, open-source agentic LLMs such as Qwen-3-Coder-480B (Qwen, 2025) and Minimax M2.5 (MiniMax AI, 2025) by recording their interactions
evidence.explanation: Names MiniMax-M2 as a teacher source for Agentic-CLI SFT trajectories.

evidence.excerpt: MiniMax-M2 (MiniMax AI, 2025) solves the obfuscated question by issuing web searches via the Tavily ... MCP search tool
evidence.explanation: MiniMax-M2 also generates search trajectories used as Search SFT data.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 generated long-context reasoning snippets used to fabricate long-input SFT samples and is one of the post-training conversational tool-use trajectory sources.

evidence.excerpt: synthetic snippets (generated using Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507) are concatenated together to form long input context
evidence.explanation: Long-context SFT samples are partly generated by Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507.

evidence.excerpt: We utilize Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507, Qwen3-32B, Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 (Yang et al., 2025), deepseek-r1-0528 ... DeepSeek-V3.2 ... and gpt-oss-120b
evidence.explanation: Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 is one of six models that generated the 279k-conversation Conversational Tool Use SFT mix.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 was used to generate Conversational Tool Use SFT trajectories.

evidence.excerpt: We utilize Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507, Qwen3-32B, Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 (Yang et al., 2025) ... on various parts of the above pipeline
evidence.explanation: Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507 named as a Conversational Tool Use SFT trajectory generator.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Qwen3-32B was one of the generators in the six-stage Conversational Tool Use synthetic SFT pipeline.

evidence.excerpt: We utilize Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507, Qwen3-32B, Qwen3-235B-A22B-Instruct-2507
evidence.explanation: Names Qwen3-32B as a Conversational Tool Use SFT generator.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Qwen3-30B-A3B was the small classifier that labelled each financial-reasoning question/answer pair ANSWERABLE/UNANSWERABLE, gating which records entered the financial-reasoning SFT dataset.

evidence.excerpt: A smaller model (Qwen3-30B-A3B) then classifies each pair as ANSWERABLE or UNANSWERABLE, retaining only those containing a complete, substantive response
evidence.explanation: Qwen3-30B-A3B decides inclusion of financial-reasoning SFT samples — a filtering role.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Qwen3-4B-Thinking-2507 performs a lightweight post-editing step on the multilingual SFT translations to restore prompt/answer format compliance after Qwen2.5-14B translation.

evidence.excerpt: we introduced a lightweight post-editing step with Qwen3-4B-Thinking-2507 to automatically restore format compliance
evidence.explanation: Names Qwen3-4B-Thinking-2507 as a rewriter/post-editor for the multilingual SFT corpus.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Qwen2.5-Instruct-14B translated English SFT examples into German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, and Chinese for the Multilingual SFT dataset (line-by-line translation pipeline reused from Nemotron 3 Nano).

evidence.excerpt: translating into six languages (German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, and Chinese) using Qwen2.5-Instruct-14b
evidence.explanation: Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct rewrites English SFT content into translations that became multilingual SFT data.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: DeepSeek-R1 was a generator (along with GPT-OSS-120B) for the synthetic CUDA-kernel SFT dataset (~100k samples covering kernel generation, repair, and optimization).

evidence.excerpt: synthetic data generation pipeline based on DeepSeek-R1 and GPT-OSS-120B
evidence.explanation: DeepSeek-R1 is named as a co-generator for the CUDA SFT dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: DeepSeek-R1-0528 generated trajectories that became part of the Conversational Tool Use SFT mix (six-stage pipeline producing 279k conversations across 838 domains).

evidence.excerpt: deepseek-r1-0528 (DeepSeek-AI, 2025b), DeepSeek-V3.2 (DeepSeek-AI, 2025a), and gpt-oss-120b (OpenAI, 2025) on various parts of the above pipeline
evidence.explanation: DeepSeek-R1-0528 is named as a generator across stages of the Conversational Tool Use pipeline.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Beyond pretraining, GPT-OSS-120B was a SFT-stage data generator: it paraphrased SecQue-derived financial templates and sampled the five candidate answers per question (judged by Qwen3-235B-A22B); was a generator in the Conversational Tool Use and CUDA pipelines; and produced low-effort-mode samples added to the SFT mix.

evidence.excerpt: GPT-OSS-120B paraphrases each template instantiation ... five candidate answers are sampled per question using GPT-OSS-120B
evidence.explanation: GPT-OSS-120B paraphrases and answer-samples for financial-reasoning SFT data.

evidence.excerpt: by adding training samples generated by GPT-OSS-120B in its low-effort mode (Du et al., 2025)
evidence.explanation: GPT-OSS-120B's low-effort generations are added to the SFT data for the new low-effort reasoning mode.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: GPT-OSS-120B served as the LLM-as-a-Judge in the Agentic CLI dataset construction, filtering out tasks that referenced pre-existing codebases or required modifications to extant files (~5k filtered out of 20k seed queries).

evidence.excerpt: we employed GPT-OSS 120B (OpenAI, 2025) in an LLM-as-a-Judge framework to filter out tasks
evidence.explanation: Documents GPT-OSS-120B as the inclusion judge for the agentic-CLI SFT dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Qwen3-235B-A22B served as the larger judge model in the GenSelect strategy used for financial-reasoning SFT data: from five GPT-OSS-120B candidate answers per question, Qwen3-235B-A22B selected the best response by numerical accuracy, methodology, and logical soundness.

evidence.excerpt: a larger judge model (Qwen3-235B-A22B) selects the best response based on numerical accuracy, financial methodology, and logical soundness
evidence.explanation: Qwen3-235B-A22B's selection determines which financial-reasoning samples were retained for SFT.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: GLM-4.7 (alongside DeepSeek-V3.2) was used to scale the General-Purpose Tool-Calling SFT pipeline, producing 1.5M diverse tool-calling trajectories.

evidence.excerpt: We scale this pipeline with DeepSeek-v3.2 (DeepSeek-AI et al., 2025) and GLM-4.7 (Z.ai / zai-org, 2025) to create a dataset of 1.5M diverse tool-calling trajectories
evidence.explanation: Names GLM-4.7 as a co-generator for the 1.5M-trajectory general-purpose tool-calling SFT dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Qwen3-Nemotron-235B-A22B-GenRM-2603 is the principle-following GenRM (initialized from Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507) used to provide reward supervision throughout the multi-environment RLVR stage and the dedicated RLHF stage; it filters which rollouts receive positive reward across both the joint multi-env and final RLHF training.

evidence.excerpt: we train using our GenRM throughout our multi environment RL stage and also perform a separate RLHF-only stage at the end of post training
evidence.explanation: GenRM-2603 supplies reward signal across both the joint RLVR and the final RLHF stage.

evidence.excerpt: Qwen3-Nemotron-235B-A22B-GenRM-2603 : GenRM used for RLHF
evidence.explanation: Authoritative naming of the released GenRM as the reward model used for RLHF.

edges.subject: nvidia/qwen3-nemotron-235b-a22b-genrm-2603
edges.description: The Nemotron 3 Super GenRM is initialized from Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 before being trained as a principle-following GenRM on HelpSteer 3 and lmarena-140k preference data.

evidence.excerpt: we use Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 as the initialization for training the GenRM
evidence.explanation: Names Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 as the GenRM initialization checkpoint.

edges.subject: nvidia/qwen3-nemotron-235b-a22b-genrm-2603
edges.description: The GenRM was trained on the HelpSteer 3 preference dataset.

evidence.excerpt: To train the GenRM we use the Helpsteer 3 dataset (Wang et al., 2025c)
evidence.explanation: HelpSteer 3 is the named GenRM training dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/qwen3-nemotron-235b-a22b-genrm-2603
edges.description: The GenRM was trained on the commercially-friendly subsets of the lmarena-140k human preference dataset (Chiang et al., 2024).

evidence.excerpt: commercially friendly subsets of the lmarena-140k dataset (Chiang et al., 2024), and some more recently collected human preference data
evidence.explanation: Names lmarena-140k subsets as GenRM training data.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Issues and containerized execution environments from SWE-Gym (Pan et al., 2025) seed the Software-Engineering SFT dataset; SWE-Gym-style tasks are also reused in the SWE-RL stage.

evidence.excerpt: We use the issues and containerized execution environments from the SWE-Gym (Pan et al., 2025), R2E-Gym (Jain et al., 2025) and SWE-rebench
evidence.explanation: Names SWE-Gym as a SWE-SFT data source.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: R2E-Gym tasks (with problem statements regenerated by Qwen3-Coder-480B) are part of the Software-Engineering SFT dataset.

evidence.excerpt: For R2E-Gym, we regenerate problem statements with Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B-Instruct
evidence.explanation: R2E-Gym is named as a SWE-SFT source whose problem statements are rewritten by Qwen3-Coder-480B.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: SWE-rebench is one of the three datasets that supply containerized GitHub-issue tasks to the Software-Engineering SFT mix.

evidence.excerpt: SWE-Gym (Pan et al., 2025), R2E-Gym (Jain et al., 2025) and SWE-rebench (Badertdinov et al., 2025)
evidence.explanation: SWE-rebench is named as a SWE-SFT source dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Glaive-Function-Calling-v2 is one of the named tool sets seeding the General-Purpose Tool-Use SFT pipeline (1.5M trajectories).

evidence.excerpt: Glaive-Function-Calling-v2 (Glaive AI, 2025), Toucan-1.5M (Xu et al., 2025), as well as custom written tools
evidence.explanation: Glaive-Function-Calling-v2 named as a foundation tool set for general-purpose tool-use SFT.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: xLAM (Salesforce APIGen Function-Calling 60k) is named as a foundation tool set for the General-Purpose Tool-Use synthetic SFT pipeline.

evidence.excerpt: AutoTools (Shi et al., 2025), xLAM (Zhang et al., 2024), Glaive-Function-Calling-v2
evidence.explanation: xLAM is named alongside other tool-set foundations for general-purpose tool-use SFT.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The Nemotron 3 Super recipe is explicitly built on top of the Nemotron 3 Nano recipe: same pretraining data mixture, two-phase curriculum, long-context phase, RLVR scaffolding (with environments expanded), RLHF flow (with GenRM), and reused SFT subsets (Chat, Infinibyte, Formal Proofs).

evidence.excerpt: We adopt the Nemotron 3 Nano data mixture as described in (NVIDIA, 2025a) ... We reuse the following datasets from the Nemotron 3 Nano SFT datasets: Chat, Infinibyte, and Formal Proofs ... We employ a unified RLVR strategy similar to Nemotron 3 Nano ... We follow a similar approach to Nemotron 3 Nano for RLHF
evidence.explanation: Multiple sections explicitly borrow Nemotron 3 Nano's data mixture, SFT subsets, RLVR strategy, and RLHF approach.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Methodology borrowing: the Safety SFT pipeline adopts the deliberative alignment framework (Guan et al., 2025) — a two-stage reasoning-trace + final-response generation procedure introduced by OpenAI for o-series safety training. (Cited as OpenAI methodology rather than via a model handle.)

evidence.excerpt: Following the deliberative alignment framework (Guan et al., 2025), we adopt a two-stage generation process
evidence.explanation: Safety SFT explicitly borrows OpenAI's deliberative-alignment methodology.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: RLVR safety environment uses the iterative attack methodology PAIR (Chao et al., 2024) to surface harder jailbreak attacks against an early SFT-only checkpoint, harvesting modified prompts that became RL training data. NOTE: PAIR is a methodology, not an artifact node — this self-edge is a placeholder for the inspired_by relation, with the real claim in the description.

evidence.excerpt: we apply an iterative attack pipeline following PAIR (Chao et al., 2024), attacking an early SFT-only checkpoint and collecting modified prompts with high attack success rates
evidence.explanation: PAIR-style iterative attack pipeline borrowed for safety RL prompt construction.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-base-bf16
edges.description: Methodology borrowing: the base partitions web crawl into five quality groups following the Nemotron-CC taxonomy (Su et al., 2025), and adopts the two-phase pretraining curriculum (Feng et al., 2024) where Phase 2 starts at 80% of training and emphasises high-quality sources.

evidence.excerpt: we partition into five quality-based groups following the Nemotron-CC taxonomy (Su et al., 2025) ... We adopt the two-phase curriculum proposed in (Feng et al., 2024) work
evidence.explanation: Two named methodologies (Nemotron-CC taxonomy + two-phase pretraining) borrowed by the base mixture design.
